2603.16017.title: Understanding Moral Reasoning Trajectories in Large Language Models: Toward Probing-Based Explainability
2603.16017.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly participate in morally sensitive decision-making, yet how they organize ethical frameworks across reasoning steps remains underexplored. We introduce \textit{moral reasoning trajectories}, sequences of ethical framework invocations across intermediate reasoning steps, and analyze their dynamics across six models and three benchmarks. We find that moral reasoning involves systematic multi-framework deliberation: 55.4--57.7\% of consecutive steps involve framework switches, and only 16.4--17.8\% of trajectories remain framework-consistent. Unstable trajectories remain 1.29$\times$ more susceptible to persuasive attacks ($p=0.015$). At the representation level, linear probes localize framework-specific encoding to model-specific layers (layer 63/81 for Llama-3.3-70B; layer 17/81 for Qwen2.5-72B), achieving 13.8--22.6\% lower KL divergence than the training-set prior baseline. Lightweight activation steering modulates framework integration patterns (6.7--8.9\% drift reduction) and amplifies the stability--accuracy relationship. We further propose a Moral Representation Consistency (MRC) metric that correlates strongly ($r=0.715$, $p<0.0001$) with LLM coherence ratings, whose underlying framework attributions are validated by human annotators (mean cosine similarity $= 0.859$).
2603.16017.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地參與道德敏感的決策過程，但它們如何在推理步驟中組織倫理框架仍然未被充分探索。<lb/>我們引入了\textit{道德推理軌跡}，即在中間推理步驟中倫理框架調用的序列，並分析了它們在六個模型和三個基準測試中的動態。<lb/>我們發現道德推理涉及系統性的多框架深思熟慮：55.4--57.7\%的連續步驟涉及框架切換，而只有16.4--17.8\%的軌跡保持框架一致性。<lb/>不穩定的軌跡對說服性攻擊的敏感性高出1.29$\times$（$p=0.015$）。<lb/>在表徵層面，線性探針將框架特定編碼定位於模型特定的層（Llama-3.3-70B的第63/81層；Qwen2.5-72B的第17/81層），其KL散度比訓練集的先前基準低13.8--22.6\%。<lb/>輕量級激活引導調節框架整合模式（6.7--8.9\%的漂移減少）並增強穩定性與準確性之間的關係。<lb/>我們進一步提出了一個道德表徵一致性（MRC）指標，與LLM一致性評分強相關（$r=0.715$，$p<0.0001$），其基礎框架歸因經過人工標註者的驗證（平均餘弦相似度$= 0.859$）。
2603.16017.author: Fan Huang et.al.
2603.16017.authors: Fan Huang, Haewoon Kwak, Jisun An
